## Purpose
This file outlines proposed future features and enhancements for the DiffDoc tool, focusing on improving functionality, scalability, reliability, search quality, developer experience, and team features.

## User-Visible Behavior
Users will see new commands and options in the DiffDoc tool, such as `diffdoc status`, `diffdoc doctor`, and enhanced summarization features. They will also experience improved reporting, error handling, and the ability to manage configurations more effectively.

## Business Rules
The document specifies rules for feature implementation, including configurable options for hash algorithms, summary styles, and concurrency limits. It also outlines requirements for metadata enrichment and CI policy checks.

## Data Inputs And Outputs
Data inputs include configuration settings, command-line arguments, and file paths. Outputs consist of summary reports, status information, and metadata exports in various formats (markdown, jsonl, csv).

## Side Effects
The proposed features may lead to changes in how data is processed and stored, including the creation of new files for summaries and checkpoints, as well as potential updates to existing indexes and configurations.

## Error And Edge Cases
The document addresses potential issues such as failed API calls, invalid configurations, and the need for retry mechanisms. It also considers edge cases like long execution times and the handling of large files.

## Dependencies
None identified.

## Operational Notes
The proposed features emphasize scalability and reliability, suggesting the use of concurrency limits, retry policies, and resumable runs. It also highlights the importance of maintaining atomic persistence and preventing concurrent execution conflicts.
